title: polypodium echinolepis
intro: Polypodium echinolepis, also known as Wallis' polypody, is a species of fern that belongs to the Polypodiaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, reducing fever, and aiding in the treatment of wounds and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, Polypodium echinolepis can be used as a tea, tincture, or infused oil, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
